summary: Interactive animated smiley face with long blinking eyelashes and eyes that follow the mouse cursor, on a dark background. The mouth reacts to mouse hover by opening and biting, then staying closed until hovered again.

existing_features.title: Create a full-screen interactive smiley face rendered using SVG or Canvas on a dark background. The face should include: a circular yellow face, two eyes with long decorative eyelashes, and a smile curve.
Implement mouse-tracking pupils: each eye's pupil should smoothly follow the position of the user's mouse cursor, constrained within the bounds of the respective eye circle.

Implement an occasional blinking animation: at random intervals (approximately every 3–6 seconds), the eyelashes and upper eyelid of both eyes should animate downward to close the eyes and then reopen, simulating a blink. The blink should last about 150–200ms.

Apply a cohesive dark visual theme throughout the app: use a near-black or very dark charcoal background, warm yellow/gold tones for the face, and a playful, high-contrast aesthetic with smooth CSS or SVG animations.

Add a mouth hover-triggered bite animation to the SVG smiley face in App.tsx. The current mouth is a static smile path (d="M 140 255 Q 200 315 260 255"). Replace it with an interactive mouth component that: (1) detects when the mouse enters the mouth region, (2) on mouse-enter, plays a quick 'open and bite' animation — the mouth opens wide (e.g., reveals an open ellipse or arc showing teeth/interior) for ~150–200ms, then snaps shut, (3) after biting, the mouth stays in a closed/flat or slightly-frowning state rather than the original smile, (4) when the mouse enters the mouth region again while it is closed/bitten, it repeats the open-bite-then-stay-closed sequence. The bite state resets only on the next mouse-enter. Use a React state variable (e.g., `mouthState: 'smile' | 'open' | 'bitten'`) and an SVG `<path>` or `<g>` to represent the three mouth states. The mouth's interactive hit area should use a transparent SVG `<ellipse>` or `<rect>` overlaid on the mouth region to reliably capture `onMouseEnter` events.

new_feature_requests.summary: Mouse hover over mouth triggers a bite animation: mouth opens and bites, then stays closed until mouse hovers over it again.
